summary: Validate a startup business model through Lean Canvas creation, customer discovery, market sizing, and financial viability assessment.

inputs.name: business_hypothesis
inputs.description: The core business hypothesis to validate.

inputs.name: target_customer
inputs.description: Description of the target customer segment.

inputs.name: market_data
inputs.description: Available market data, competitive info, or early traction metrics.

outputs.name: lean_canvas
outputs.description: Completed Lean Canvas with validated hypotheses.

outputs.name: validation_report
outputs.description: Summary of validation findings with go/no-go recommendation.

outputs.name: next_steps
outputs.description: Prioritized list of next validation activities.

gates.rule: Confirm the task is business model validation, not product feature design.
Require business hypothesis and target customer before proceeding.
Output must include a clear go/no-go recommendation with evidence.

steps.action: Create Lean Canvas from business hypothesis and initial assumptions.
Design customer interview plan to test problem-solution fit.
Calculate TAM/SAM/SOM and map competitive landscape.
Assess unit economics and financial viability of the model.
Synthesize findings into validation report with recommendation.

tools.name: filesystem.read
tools.purpose: Read existing business documents, metrics, and prior analysis.

verification.method: Check Lean Canvas has all sections filled with evidence.
Check that customer discovery findings are cited.

success_criteria: Lean Canvas is complete with evidence-backed entries.
Customer discovery findings support or refute key hypotheses.
Market sizing provides realistic opportunity assessment.

rollback.when: If outputs invent metrics or misrepresent findings, roll back to raw inputs.
